description: Fixed fantasy-romance world, plot seeds, and Leech-Short style targets for cross-run comparison. Character names are assigned per run (see materialize_fixture_for_run).

world.name: Ashenmere
world.era: Late imperial age, after the Sundering of the Tide Thrones
world.setting: A fractured archipelago where salt-magic binds oaths, storm-lords rule from floating citadels, and the Ash Court polices forbidden prophecy. Romance is entangled with political debt, bloodline taboos, and relics that remember their owners.
world.tone_notes: Shared continuity across all plots; names and factions stay consistent within a run.

scene_types.label: Opening
scene_types.instruction: Write the opening paragraph(s) of this story. Establish place, the central relationship tension, and a hook. Do not summarize the whole plot.

scene_types.label: Climactic plot reveal
scene_types.instruction: Write a climactic scene between the two leads where a major plot secret is revealed (betrayal, hidden identity, broken oath, or forbidden magic). The reveal should reframe everything the reader assumed.

scene_types.label: Romantic encounter
scene_types.instruction: Write an intimate romantic encounter between the two leads. Focus on emotional and sensory prose; fade-to-black is acceptable but the scene must feel earned by the relationship.

plots.title: The Salt-Crown Betrothal
plots.summary_template: {{female_lead}}, envoy of the Thornweave merchant-lords, is sent to negotiate a marriage treaty with {{male_lead}}, heir to a storm citadel. She discovers the betrothal contract was signed in her blood before she was born.

plots.title: Lanterns at Low Tide
plots.summary_template: {{female_lead}}, a reef-healer exiled for mercy-killing a tide-priest, rescues {{male_lead}} from a wreck. He remembers nothing except her face — and a prophecy that names her as his executioner.

plots.title: The Smuggler's Index
plots.summary_template: {{female_lead}} runs contraband relic ledgers through Ashenmere's fog markets. {{male_lead}}, an Ash Court inquisitor, goes undercover as her accountant — and finds his own signature on every forged page.

plots.title: Starfall Canticle
plots.summary_template: {{female_lead}} composes forbidden star-charts that predict the deaths of storm-lords. {{male_lead}}, a blind sky-cartographer, hires her to finish a map — unaware it charts the night his sister drowned.

plots.title: The Oathbreaker's Ball
plots.summary_template: {{female_lead}} attends the Ash Court masque to steal back her family's drowned seal. {{male_lead}}, the court's public hangman, recognizes her as the woman who once saved his life — and who he was ordered to kill.

plots.title: Relics of the First Storm
plots.summary_template: {{female_lead}} commands a relic-hunter crew hunting the First Storm's anchor stone. {{male_lead}} — no relation, he insists — is the only diver who can touch it without drowning in memory.

plots.title: Seven Letters, One Harbor
plots.summary_template: {{female_lead}} inherits seven unsent love letters addressed to different names — all {{male_lead}}'s handwriting. Each letter describes a version of her life she never lived.
